revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=久留里神社| |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=千葉県}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][君津市]]浦田14番地1号| 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 17|緯度秒 = 21| 経度度 =140 |経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 58| |祭神=[[天御中主命]]
[[伊邪那美神]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[応神天皇]]など| |社格=旧郷社| |創建=[[治安]]元年(1021年)| |例祭=[[7月22日]]| }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]と[[伊邪那美神]]、[[天照皇大神]]、[[少彦名命]]、[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたとという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている（君津市史・金石文編469p） [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏、土屋氏、酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。 最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフににした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市史　君津市市史編さん委員会編 *千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城誌　　久留里城再建協力会 *久留里城物語　　府馬　清著 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:はくさんしんしや きみつし}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:久留里神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

contributor.username: ja>播磨国の鉄道・音楽ファン

{{神社| 名称=久留里神社| |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=千葉県}} |所在地=[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田14番地1号| 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 17|緯度秒 = 21| 経度度 =140 |経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 58| |祭神=[[天御中主命]]
[[伊邪那美神]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[応神天皇]]など| |社格=旧郷社| |創建=[[治安]]元年(1021年)| |例祭=[[7月22日]]| }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたとという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている（君津市史・金石文編469p） [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。 最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフににした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市史　君津市市史編さん委員会編 *千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城誌　　久留里城再建協力会 *久留里城物語　　府馬　清著 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

[[応神天皇]]など| |社格=旧郷社| |創建=[[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年(1021年)| |例祭=[[7月22日]]| }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたとという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている（君津市史・金石文編469p） [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。 最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフにした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市史　君津市市史編さん委員会編 *千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城誌　　久留里城再建協力会 *久留里城物語　　府馬　清著 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

[[応神天皇]]など| |社格=旧郷社| |創建=[[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年(1021年)| |例祭=[[7月22日]]| }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている（君津市史・金石文編469p） [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。 最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフにした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市史　君津市市史編さん委員会編 *千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城誌　　久留里城再建協力会 *久留里城物語　　府馬　清著 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]]

[[応神天皇]]など | 社格=旧郷社 | 創建=[[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年(1021年) | 例祭=[[7月22日]] }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている（君津市史・金石文編469p） [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇|應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフにした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Kururijinja.jpg|サムネイル|久留里神社拝殿]] （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。拝殿は、2019年の台風15号で倒壊した。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市市史編さん委員会編『君津市史』 *千葉県企画部文化国際課編『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城再建協力会『久留里城誌』　 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]]

{{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | Image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | Deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | Annual festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) [[ja:久留里神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[Category:dd]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[Category:dd]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia19]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[Category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}[[Category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[Category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} [[ja:久留里神社]]

Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) [[ja:久留里神社]] [[category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:Former Gōsha]] [[category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}[[category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} [[ja:久留里神社]]

{{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | Image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | Deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | Annual festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) [[ja:久留里神社]] [[category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[category:Former Gōsha]] [[category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]][[category:pages and categories from Wikipedia]] [[category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}[[category:Myōken Faith articles that need translation]] [[category:original pages]] ==References== {{reflist}} [[ja:久留里神社]]

{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | Annual festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | Annual festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == Annual Festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | Name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Founded = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox Shrine | name = Kururi Shrine | image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) revision.comment: forking the category so massreplace can work better on it, fixing category typo
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shrine | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information)
