revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』== See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|12=draft}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|12=draft}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and others | Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|12=draft}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New4]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kururi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:久留里神社]] [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|12=draft}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
