revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:久留里神社 up to rev 99509462
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=久留里神社 | 画像 =[[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
鳥居 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | 所在地=[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田14番地1号 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 17|緯度秒 = 21 | 経度度 =140 |経度分 = 4|経度秒 = 58 | 祭神=[[天御中主命]]
[[伊邪那美神]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[応神天皇]]など | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年(1021年) | 例祭=[[7月22日]] }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。また[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている『君津市史　金石文編』469p。 [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇|應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフにした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Kururijinja.jpg|サムネイル|久留里神社拝殿]] （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。拝殿は、2019年の台風15号で倒壊した。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市市史編さん委員会編『君津市史』 *千葉県企画部文化国際課編『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城再建協力会『久留里城誌』　 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]]

[[応神天皇]]など | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年(1021年) | 例祭=[[7月22日]] }} '''久留里神社'''（くるりじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[君津市]]浦田（[[上総国]][[望陀郡]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == [[天御中主命]]・[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇]]などを祀る。 == 由緒 == [[治安 (元号)|治安]]元年（1021）、久留里城主上総介[[平忠常]]が浦田妙見堂として勧請、別名を細田妙見という。[[平将門]]が勧請して「細田妙見」と称し、将門は天女を 妻として、子を産ませたという伝説もある。[[建久]]3年(1192)7月22日、[[源頼朝]]も真鶴より勝山に上陸した時に戦勝祈願し、天下統一後[[征夷大将軍]]となるや[[文覚]]の弟子[[頼忠]]上人を別当としてお礼の祭りを執り行ったことから、7月22日を神社の大祭日としている。 治安年間に創建された当時の場所は、現在地に隣接する場所にあり、細田と称されている。また[[久留里藩]]の家老を務めた[[森家]]によってその事跡を顕彰した碑が神社の隣接地に建てられている『君津市史　金石文編』469p。 [[武田真勝]]や[[里見義堯]]をはじめ、江戸時代には大須賀氏・土屋氏・酒井氏などの領主の信仰も篤く、[[享保]]2年(1717)に入封した[[黒田直純]]によって久留里城築城の願文が奉納され、以後歴代藩主が崇敬してきた。大正元年9月に[[日本武尊]]・[[市杵島比賣命]]・[[木花之開耶比賣命]]・[[大己貴命]]を、同2年6月に[[伊邪那美神]]・[[天照皇大神]]・[[少彦名命]]・[[応神天皇|應神天皇]]を、同年9月には[[菅原道真命]]を本社に合祀。さらに[[昭和]]3年近隣の十社を合祀してその総社となった。 ==その他== *久留里は[[環境省]]の平成の名水百選の町にも選ばれている名水地で、神社の境内でも御神水として水が湧き出ている。最近ではパワースポットとしてこの御神水を求める観光客も多い。 *[[妙見]]とは[[北極星]]を神格化したもので、その姿は亀に乗った武人であるとされていることから、境内には亀をモチーフにした像が多く見られる。 *平将門の三男頼胤が細田妙見を参詣の際、「城は浦田山に築き、久しくこの里に留まるべし」との御託宣があったとされ、これが久留里という地名の発祥になったという説もある。 ==例大祭== *例大祭のうち、神事は7月22日に行われるが、近年は神輿渡御については必ずしも例大祭日とはならず、7月22日前後の土曜日に「久留里夏祭り」として開催されている。 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Kururijinja.jpg|サムネイル|久留里神社拝殿]] （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） *久留里神社本殿　（昭和50年8月4日に市指定文化財〔建造物〕に指定） 神仏習合時代の名残がうかがわれる建築物として評価されている。拝殿は、2019年の台風15号で倒壊した。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[久留里線]][[久留里駅]]下車徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *君津市市史編さん委員会編『君津市史』 *千葉県企画部文化国際課編『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 千葉県企画部文化国際課編 *久留里城再建協力会『久留里城誌』　 == 関連項目 == * [[久留里藩]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html 久留里神社本殿]（君津市役所「君津市観光情報」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くるりしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}

{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|12=draft}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|12=draft}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|12=draft}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=draft}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=draft}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=draft}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|12=draft}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=draft}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|12=draft}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qq=}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Line|qq=}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Domain|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimitsu City|qq=}} Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chian era|qq=}} 1 (1021) | festival = {{ill|July 22|ja|7月22日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 22|qq=}} }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Urata, {{ill|Kimitsu City|ja|君津市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimitsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in {{ill|Chian era|ja|治安 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chian era|qq=}} 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as {{ill|Shōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgun|qq=}}, he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Domain|qq=}}, stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Satomi Yoshitaka|ja|里見義堯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yoshitaka|qq=}}, and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|ja|環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|lt=Ministry of the Environment|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qq=}} as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kururi Station|ja|久留里駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kururi Line|ja|久留里線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Line|qq=}} (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * {{ill|Kururi Domain|ja|久留里藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kururi Domain|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Kururi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]]

In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:君津市の建築物]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[en:Kururi Shrine]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kimitsu City]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]
