[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 2]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津市の歴史]]

Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 2]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:君津の Municipal History]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 2]] }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kimitsu]]

There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kimitsu]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kururi Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|17|21|N|140|4|58|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kimitsu]]

{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and others | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] }} '''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others. == History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day. Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.『君津市史 金石文編』469p Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here. == Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area. == festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival." == Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] (Designation type and date in parentheses) * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East) == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』 == See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]] == External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information) ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|久留里神社|version=99509462|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Kururi Shrine]] [[ja:久留里神社]] [[de:Kururi Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kimitsu, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kimitsu]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留里神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22118623]] Wikidata name: Kururi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kururi Jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] | location = {{ill|Kimitsu|qid=Q637928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.289167|N|140.082778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | founded_by = {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|qid=Q1105844}} }}
'''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others.
== History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day.
Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.<ref>『君津市史 金石文編』469p</ref>
Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』
== See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information)
==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}
==References== {{reflist}}

Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留里神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118623]] Wikidata name: Kururi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kururi Jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] | location = {{ill|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.289167|N|140.082778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | founded_by = {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844}} }}
'''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others.
== History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day.
Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.<ref>『君津市史 金石文編』469p</ref>
Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|qq=|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留里神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118623]] Wikidata name: Kururi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kururi Jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] | location = {{ill|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.289167|N|140.082778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | founded_by = {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844}} }}
'''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others.
== History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day.
Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.<ref>『君津市史 金石文編』469p</ref>
Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』
== See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information)
==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}
==References== {{reflist}}

In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留里神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118623]] Wikidata name: Kururi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kururi Jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] | location = {{ill|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.289167|N|140.082778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | founded_by = {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844}} }}
'''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others.
== History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day.
Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.<ref>『君津市史 金石文編』469p</ref>
Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久留里神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118623]] Wikidata name: Kururi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kururi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kururi Jinja.JPG | location = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]] Urata 14-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude minutes = 17 | Latitude seconds = 21 | Longitude = 140 | Longitude minutes = 4 | Longitude seconds = 58 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Izanami|WD=Q682306}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year = [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) | festival = [[:en:July 22|July 22]] | location = {{ill|Kimitsu|WD=Q637928}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.289167|N|140.082778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | founded_by = {{ill|Taira no Tadatsune|WD=Q1105844}} }}
'''Kururi Shrine''' (久留里神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Urata, [[:en:Kimitsu|Kimitsu City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and others.
== History == Kururi Shrine was established in [[:en:Jian (era)|Chian era]] 1 (1021) by Taira no Tadatsune, the lord of Kururi Castle, as Urata Myōkendō. It was also known as Hosoda Myōken. There is a legend that {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined the deity and called it "Hosoda Myōken," claiming that he married a celestial maiden who bore him a child. On July 22, 1192, [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], after landing at Katsuyama from Manazuru, prayed for victory at this shrine. Following his unification of Japan and appointment as [[:en:Shogun|Shōgun]], he instructed his disciple, Monk Raitada, to hold a festival of gratitude on this date, which became the shrine's major festival day.
Initially located adjacent to its current site, the shrine was referred to as Hosoda. A monument commemorating its history, erected by the Mori family, who served as chief retainers of the [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]], stands near the shrine.<ref>『君津市史 金石文編』469p</ref>
Devoutly worshiped by warlords including {{ill|Takeda Masakatsu|ja|武田真勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Masakatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Satomi Yoshitaka|Satomi Yoshitaka]], and feudal rulers such as the Ōsuka, Tsuchiya, and Sakai clans during the Edo period, the shrine also received patronage from Kururi Domain lords. In 1717, {{ill|Kuroda Naozumi|ja|黒田直純|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Naozumi|1=Kuroda Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10518200}} dedicated a vow for the construction of Kururi Castle, and subsequent domain lords continued this reverence. In the early {{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1912年|1=1912|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2077}}, various deities, including {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花之開耶比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were enshrined here.
== Additional Notes == * Kururi is celebrated for its water quality, being selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the "Heisei Famous Water Hundred." Sacred water springs within the shrine grounds attract visitors seeking blessings at this power spot. * The shrine features many turtle motifs, reflecting the belief that {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10960623}}, a deified representation of the North Star, rides a turtle. * According to legend, Masakado’s third son, Yoritane, received a divine message while visiting the shrine, leading to the construction of a castle in Urata and the naming of the Kururi area.
== festival == The annual festival includes rituals held on July 22. However, in recent years, the portable shrine procession has been conducted on the nearest Saturday, celebrated as the "Kururi Summer Festival."
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Kururijinja.jpg|thumb|Kururi Shrine Worship Hall]] <small>(Designation type and date in parentheses)</small> * **Kururi Shrine Main Hall** (Designated as a Municipal Cultural Property, Building, on August 4, 1975): The hall retains remnants of the shrine’s historical syncretism with Buddhism. The worship hall collapsed during Typhoon No. 15 in 2019.
== Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kururi Station|Kururi Station]] on the [[:en:Kururi Line|Kururi Line]] (JR East)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kimitsu City History Compilation Committee, 『君津市史』 * Cultural Affairs Division, Chiba Prefecture, 『千葉県史料 近世篇 久留里藩制一班』 * Kururi Castle Reconstruction Support Association, 『久留里城誌』
== See Also == * [[:en:Kururi Domain|Kururi Domain]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kiminavi.jp/detail/index_35.html Kururi Shrine Main Hall] (Kimitsu City Official Tourism Information)
==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}}
==References== {{reflist}}
